articles.headline: Airline stocks hit turbulence amid Mideast conflict: Latest news
articles.summary: US airline stocks faced slight headwinds in Wednesday's trading session in response to the recent escalations between the US and Iran, compounded by the jolt in oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F). Market Domination Overtime Host Josh Lipton is joined by Yahoo Finance Senior Reporters Brooke DiPalma and Pras Subramanian for a chat about the latest headlines emerging from the airline industry today, including United Airlines' (UAL) new headphone rule and the airport lounge American Express (AXP) opened in Las Vegas. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination Overtime.

articles.headline: American Airlines Miami Bet Tests Valuation Case And Geopolitical Resilience
articles.summary: American Airlines Group announced a $1b investment to expand and upgrade its Miami International Airport hub. The project is aimed at reinforcing Miami as a key gateway for U.S. and Latin America travel. At the same time, the airline is dealing with operational disruptions and cost pressures linked to geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. For investors watching NasdaqGS:AAL, this mix of large capital spending and external pressure comes against a challenging recent share price history...

articles.headline: Americans in the Middle East Urged to Get Home. Where That Leaves U.S. Airlines.
articles.summary: The U.S. government is urging Americans stranded in the Middle East to get home via commercial means. That’s easier said than done.

articles.headline: American Airlines to present at 2026 J.P. Morgan Industrials Conference
articles.summary: FORT WORTH, Texas, March 03, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- American Airlines Group Inc. (NASDAQ: AAL) will present at the 2026 J.P. Morgan Industrials Conference on March 17 at 8:10 a.m. ET. A live webcast of the conference will be available at aa.com/investorrelations. About American Airlines Group (NASDAQ: AAL)American Airlines is a premium global airline connecting more of the U.S. to the world. articles.headline: Airline Stocks Extend Losses as Carriers Weigh Disruptions, Higher Fuel Costs
articles.summary: Shares in American Airlines, Delta Air Lines and United Airlines all fell in premarket trading.  European carriers, including Air France-KLM, British Airways owner IAG and Deutsche Lufthansa dropped.  El Al Israel Airlines dropped nearly 5% in Tel Aviv.

articles.headline: Shares of airline stocks are trading lower amid growing concerns over escalating conflict in the Middle East and its impact on energy costs. Investors are reacting to the spike in oil prices and the potential effect on jet fuel costs.
